body: With this, now I can plot via the extension with CairoMakie v0.15 ```Julia julia> using Pkg; Pkg.status() Status `~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TheUniversityofMelbourne/Documents/Research/Oceananigans.jl-v5/docs/Project.toml` [79e6a3ab] Adapt v4.4.0 [052768ef] CUDA v5.9.4 [13f3f980] CairoMakie v0.15.6 [e30172f5] Documenter v1.16.0 [daee34ce] DocumenterCitations v1.4.1 [033835bb] JLD2 v0.6.2 [98b081ad] Literate v2.20.1 [da04e1cc] MPI v0.20.23 [85f8d34a] NCDatasets v0.14.10 [9e8cae18] Oceananigans v0.101.2 `~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TheUniversityofMelbourne/Documents/Research/Oceananigans.jl-v5` [f27b6e38] Polynomials v4.1.0 [6038ab10] Rotations v1.7.1 [d496a93d] SeawaterPolynomials v0.3.10 [09ab397b] StructArrays v0.7.2 [bdfc003b] TimesDates v0.3.3 [2e0b0046] XESMF v0.1.6 [37e2e46d] LinearAlgebra v1.12.0 julia> using Oceananigans, CairoMakie [ Info: Oceananigans will use 12 threads julia> grid = RectilinearGrid(size=(8, 6), x=(0, 2π), y=(0, 2π), topology=(Periodic, Periodic, Flat)) julia> c = CenterField(grid); julia> set!(c, (x, y) -> rand()); julia> heatmap(view(c, :, :, 1)) ```
